title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: kagiPro R-first client Kagi APIs designed reproducible data workflows. package optimized users want : Build structured API requests R scripts. Run single batch requests uniform way. Persist raw API responses JSON traceability. Convert JSON outputs parquet analytics pipelines. document intended AI assistants (example Codex, Claude Code, similar tools) changes stay aligned project intent.

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Query-first architecture. Users construct explicit query objects first, execute . Query construction execution separate concerns. Endpoint-specific constructors, shared execution engine. Constructors encode endpoint semantics; kagi_request() executes supported query objects. Reproducibility implicit behavior. Requests write JSON files disk. File-based outputs treated source--truth artifacts. Query replay metadata persisted per query (_query_meta.json) support deterministic re-runs. Predictable scaling single batch. Constructors return named lists consistently one-query many-query workflows use calling pattern. Pragmatic fault tolerance. error_mode = "stop" strict runs; error_mode = "write_dummy" long pipelines partial completion preferred. Analysis handoff first-class workflow. kagi_request_parquet() converts JSON collections parquet downstream processing. Modular enrichment pipeline. Content download, markdown extraction, abstract generation separate steps (download_content() -> content_markdown() -> markdown_abstract()).

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Expected user flow: Create connection kagi_connection(). Build query objects using endpoint-specific constructors. Preferred: execute kagi_fetch() endpoint-scoped project folders. Low-level alternative: kagi_request() + kagi_request_parquet(). contract remain stable across releases.

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Current endpoint constructors: query_search() query_enrich_web() query_enrich_news() query_summarize() query_fastgpt() kagi_fetch() (high-level orchestrator) constructors : Return named lists. Preserve endpoint-specific parameters without hidden coercion. accepted directly kagi_request().

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: kagi_request() supports two modes: error_mode = "stop": Fail fast raise error. error_mode = "write_dummy": Emit warning write structured dummy JSON payload. Dummy payload requirements: Include metadata failed request. Keep endpoint shape compatible possible. Use empty/null data fields missing payloads. Remain convertible kagi_request_parquet().

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: JSON files audit artifacts. Parquet derivative format analysis. Design implications: Never require parquet conversion core package usage. Prefer non-lossy conversion possible. Handle partial failures dummy payloads without breaking conversion. Keep query partitions (query=<name>) independently refreshable.

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Testing cassette-driven vcr ensure deterministic API behavior CI local re-runs. Priorities: Cover exported constructors request paths. Include failure-path tests, especially dummy-write mode. Validate parquet conversion mixed success/error outputs. Keep fixture layout consistent (tests/testthat/fixtures/cassettes).

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Documentation must user-oriented narrative, just code snippets. Required doc layers: README onboarding package overview. Quickstart vignette first end--end run. Endpoint vignettes deep usage patterns. Changelog-style document release deltas. Narrative guidelines: Explain showing code. Keep examples realistic pipeline-oriented. Include error-handling production guidance.

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Agent-oriented operational guidance packaged inst/skills. maintainer-workflow covers implementation conventions, tests/cassettes, naming, release hygiene. maintainer-corpus-pipeline covers content/markdown/abstract internals corpus-link contracts. maintainer-release-sync covers pre-release consistency checks across code, docs, vignettes, skills, changelog. Endpoint user skills (user-search, user-enrich, user-summarize, user-fastgpt) mirror endpoint vignettes. user-corpus-workflow mirrors end--end corpus vignette (vignettes/corpus-workflow.qmd). Skills intended strict execution guidance coding agents must remain aligned package behavior vignette examples.

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Preferred skill workflow phase: Endpoint query construction request execution: use user-search, user-enrich, user-summarize, user-fastgpt depending endpoint. End--end corpus build (parquet -> content -> markdown -> abstract): use user-corpus-workflow. Internal pipeline changes (download/extraction/summarization/linking): use maintainer-corpus-pipeline. Cross-cutting package changes (API behavior, tests, docs contracts): use maintainer-workflow. Pre-release / merge final synchronization: use maintainer-release-sync.

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Key project-level changes reflected cycle: Standardized query constructor behavior named lists. query_search() query_enrich_web() query_enrich_news() query_summarize() query_fastgpt() Added/expanded FastGPT endpoint support via query_fastgpt(). Added graceful fallback behavior kagi_request() dummy outputs. Added query replay metadata files (_query_meta.json) written kagi_request() single source truth. Ensured dummy outputs can flow parquet conversion. Added kagi_update_query() query-name scoped reruns parquet partition refresh. Added clean_request() remove JSON request artifacts preserving per-query metadata later reruns. Strengthened tests around mixed success/failure request lists. Consolidated cassette location vcr helper setup. Updated quickstart added endpoint-focused vignettes. Aligned pkgdown output article organization. Replaced legacy abstract augmentation modular corpus pipeline: download_content() -> content_markdown() -> markdown_abstract(). Added pluggable content summarizers: summarize_with_openai() summarize_with_kagi(). Added read_corpus() optional abstract linking (abstracts = TRUE) id + query. Added AI-agent skills inst/skills maintainer endpoint-specific workflows. Added standard disclaimer AI-assisted development notice README. Removed unused legacy assets inst/ (old extdata, plantuml diagrams, query_test script).

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: modifying repository, AI agents : Preserve query-constructor -> request-execution separation. Avoid introducing endpoint-specific logic generic file/IO paths unless required. Keep strict graceful error behavior tested. Prefer additive changes query schema; flag breaking parameter renames explicitly. Update tests, vignettes, changelog change set. Treat JSON output compatibility stability concern.

title: kagiPro Design Philosophy and Project Context (for AI Coding Agents)
text: Potential next items evaluate: Additional Kagi endpoints beyond current coverage. explicit schema versioning dummy payloads. Optional validation helpers API key / endpoint health checks. Tighter invariants parquet schema across endpoint versions.

title: kagiPro Corpus Workflow: Search to Abstract-Linked Corpus
text: vignette shows one complete, reproducible workflow focused Search endpoint: build multiple search queries, fetch JSON project folder, convert parquet, download linked content, convert content markdown, summarize markdown abstract parquet files, read final corpus read_corpus(abstracts = TRUE). goal create corpus ready downstream vector/comparison workflows preserving query-level partitions. Using named list keeps query names stable throughout JSON/parquet/content partitions (query=<query_name>). writes: tests_complex/search/json tests_complex/search/parquet run kagi_request() manually instead kagi_fetch(), convert JSON parquet explicitly: Download search queries endpoint: writes binary/source files : tests_complex/search/content/query=<query_name>/... writes markdown files : tests_complex/search/markdown/query=<query_name>/... Use OpenAI summarization: use Kagi summarization extracted text: Abstract parquet files written : tests_complex/search/abstract/query=<query_name>/... Read parquet : Read parquet linked abstracts (id + query): stage, tbl query-partitioned search corpus additional abstract column, ready downstream modeling comparison workflows. Keep query names stable; update/rebuild unit. download_content(), content_markdown(), markdown_abstract() support selector expansion (endpoint = NULL /query_name = NULL). read_corpus(abstracts = TRUE) expects abstract parquet schema lowercase abstract.
code: library(kagiPro)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") )  q <- c(   biodiversity_search = query_search(     query = "biodiversity annual report",     filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),     expand = FALSE   )[[1]],   ecosystem_methods = query_search(     query = "ecosystem services valuation methods",     filetype = c("pdf", "docx"),     expand = FALSE   )[[1]] ) project_folder <- "tests_complex"  kagi_fetch(   connection = conn,   query = q,   project_folder = project_folder,   overwrite = TRUE ) kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = file.path(project_folder, "search", "json"),   output = file.path(project_folder, "search", "parquet"),   overwrite = TRUE ) download_content(   project_folder = project_folder,   endpoint = "search",   query_name = NULL,   # all queries in `search`   workers = 4 ) content_markdown(   project_folder = project_folder,   endpoint = "search",   query_name = NULL,   # all queries in `search`   workers = 4 ) markdown_abstract(   project_folder = project_folder,   endpoint = "search",   query_name = NULL,   # all queries in `search`   summarizer_fn = summarize_with_openai,   model = "gpt-4.1-mini",   workers = 1          # sequential is recommended for OpenAI rate limits ) markdown_abstract(   project_folder = project_folder,   endpoint = "search",   query_name = NULL,   summarizer_fn = summarize_with_kagi,   model = "cecil",   connection = conn,   workers = 4 ) ds <- read_corpus(   project_folder = project_folder,   endpoint = "search",   corpus = "parquet",   abstracts = FALSE ) ds_abs <- read_corpus(   project_folder = project_folder,   endpoint = "search",   corpus = "parquet",   abstracts = TRUE,   silent = TRUE )  tbl <- dplyr::collect(ds_abs) names(tbl)

what: Enrich Endpoint: Context Discovery for Web and News

title: kagiPro Summarize Endpoint Guide
text: Universal Summarizer endpoint often users need quality resilience. practice, summarization workloads mixed: inputs ideal, short, fail upstream reasons outside script. guide shows keep pipelines reliable preserving useful output. kagiPro supports URL-based raw-text summarization. calls return named lists, execution stays consistent regardless input style. baseline path standard inputs. short text can trigger server-side failures (example minimum document size constraints). want full pipeline stop, use graceful mode. mode, request warns writes dummy JSON payload, summarize fields present empty (data$output = null, data$tokens = 0). realistic batch often contains valid invalid inputs. keeps successful summary recording structured placeholder failed item. Parquet conversion allows downstream analysis retaining run-level consistency. Use meaningful minimum text lengths sending summarize requests. Use workers = 1 diagnosing warnings, scale . Prefer error_mode = "write_dummy" long unattended jobs.
code: library(kagiPro)  conn <- kagi_connection(   api_key = function() keyring::key_get("API_kagi") ) q_url <- query_summarize(   url = "https://www.example.com/long-article",   engine = "muriel",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN",   cache = TRUE ) q_text <- query_summarize(   text = paste(     "Biodiversity underpins ecosystem services such as pollination, soil fertility,",     "water purification, and climate regulation.",     "Habitat loss and climate pressure accelerate species decline with consequences",     "for resilience and human wellbeing."   ),   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "takeaway",   target_language = "EN",   cache = TRUE ) out_sum <- "summarize_results" dir.create(out_sum, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_text[[1]],   output = out_sum,   overwrite = TRUE ) q_short <- query_summarize(   text = "Too short.",   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = q_short[[1]],   output = "summarize_short_safe",   overwrite = TRUE,   error_mode = "write_dummy" ) q_ok <- query_summarize(   text = paste(rep("Long summarize input text.", 40), collapse = " "),   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  q_err <- query_summarize(   text = "short",   engine = "cecil",   summary_type = "summary",   target_language = "EN" )  kagi_request(   connection = conn,   query = list(ok = q_ok[[1]], err = q_err[[1]]),   output = "summarize_mixed",   overwrite = TRUE,   workers = 1,   error_mode = "write_dummy" ) kagi_request_parquet(   input_json = "summarize_mixed",   output = "summarize_mixed_parquet",   overwrite = TRUE )

title: kagiPro 0.4.0
text: Added kagi_fetch() high-level project-folder workflow helper endpoint-scoped outputs (<project>/<endpoint>/json <project>/<endpoint>/parquet). Added endpoint-specific query support FastGPT via query_fastgpt(). download_content() content_markdown() markdown_abstract() endpoint/query partitioned output <project>/<endpoint>/{content,markdown,abstract}. summarize_with_openai() summarize_with_kagi() (text-based summarizer usage). Added read_corpus() optional abstract linking (abstracts = TRUE) id + query. Standardized query constructor outputs named lists across endpoints. Extended kagi_request() error_mode ("stop", "write_dummy") strict vs resilient execution modes. Improved handling mixed query lists long batch runs can continue structured dummy outputs failure. Kept JSON--parquet first-class path via kagi_request_parquet() downstream analytics. Added low-level 00_in.progress marker lifecycle request/parquet runs folder-state visibility long jobs. per-query _query_meta.json Added kagi_update_query() rerun query_name across matching endpoints refresh touched parquet query=<name> partitions. Added clean_request() remove JSON request artifacts project-wide preserving _query_meta.json per query (dry_run space estimates). maintainer-workflow user-search, user-enrich, user-summarize, user-fastgpt

title: kagiPro 0.4.0
text: Fixed inconsistent request handling single-query list-query execution paths. Fixed recursive/list request execution propagate request options (including error handling mode) correctly. Fixed output path behavior single-item list requests create unexpected nested output directories. Removed redundant API key resolution calls connection flow applicable. Fixed fixture-location inconsistencies tests using single cassette location.

title: kagiPro 0.4.0
text: search_query() -> query_search() enrich_web_query() -> query_enrich_web() enrich_news_query() -> query_enrich_news() summarize_query() -> query_summarize() fastgpt_query() -> query_fastgpt() Removed add_sbstract_to_parquet(). Abstract creation now handled via modular content pipeline (download_content() -> content_markdown() -> markdown_abstract()). kagi_request_parquet() JSON--parquet conversion longer accepts abstract-augmentation arguments. Query constructors consistently return named lists. Code previously assumed bare single query object may require [[1]] indexing direct calls. Error behavior can now configured explicitly; strict failure remains default, resilient mode writes dummy payloads warnings instead stopping.

title: kagiPro 0.4.0

Updated pkgdown article structure clearer endpoint-based navigation. Added project-level design/context documentation maintainers AI coding agents (PROJECT_DESIGN.md).
